problem: Why does the immune system attack the body's own tissue in autoimmune disease?
mechanism: Small molecule inhibitor of enzyme active site
hypothesis: Small molecule inhibitor of enzyme active site may resolve Why does the immune system attack the body's own tissue in autoimmune disease? by blocking at the molecular level.

experiment.protocol: Human organoid model with 32 month follow-up. Control group receives placebo/sham.

wisdom: To know that you do not know is the best. — Lao Tzu
